rawContent: {"stage":"build","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"When a route is wrapped with withAuth HOC, the handler is never called with user=null. Any `if (!user)` guards are dead code and should be removed.","scope":"auth middleware"},{"kind":"convention","text":"The codebase uses withAuth HOC (src/auth/withAuth.ts) to protect routes, not inline middleware. All protected routes under src/app/api/* must be wrapped.","scope":"auth"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Always check existing routes before planning route-level changes — routes may already be protected by existing wrappers, making the task only about cleanup.","scope":"route protection"}]}
